page: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-48xl is a bare metal, storage-optimized server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor family running at 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the server includes 45000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores ranking in the top tier. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, reaching 871.74 GB/sec for large block reads and 403.47 GB/sec for large block writes. In database and storage-related tasks, the server achieves top-tier performance, executing 347.98 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and scoring in the top tier for PassMark database operations. However, single-core performance is moderate, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core scores falling in the average tier. LLM text generation performance is also limited, registering in the poor and weak tiers for small and medium models respectively.

The server offers high resource density, combining massive local NVMe SSD storage with a high-core arm64 processor and DDR5 memory. This hardware profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-throughput local storage and substantial memory capacity without the overhead of virtualization. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks such as software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare metal server featuring 192 dedicated arm64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a massive 45000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, alongside exceptional database throughput, including 347.98 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. While single-core and LLM text generation performance remain average to weak, the server's high resource density makes it highly efficient for large-scale database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and intensive multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare metal server with 192 arm64 cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, 45000 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A high-density bare metal server combining 192 arm64 cores with massive local NVMe storage and top-tier memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated arm64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
45000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory with top-tier bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and static web serving
